description: Git version control and GitHub CLI (gh) commands

categories.name: Git Basics
categories.description: Everyday git commands for version control

examples.description: Compact branch graph for all branches
Show last 10 commits with diffs
Find commits by a specific author

commands.name: git diff
commands.summary: Show changes between commits, branches, or working tree

options.description: Summary of changed files and line counts

examples.description: See what's staged for the next commit
Compare current branch with main
List files changed between commits

options.description: Apply the most recent stash and remove it
Apply a specific stash without removing it

examples.description: Stash everything including untracked files
Pop the latest stash

commands.name: git reset

options.description: Undo N commits, keep changes staged
Undo N commits, keep changes unstaged (default)
Undo N commits, DISCARD all changes

examples.description: Undo last commit but keep changes staged
Unstage a file

commands.note: --hard permanently discards changes. Make sure you don't need them before running.

commands.name: git cherry-pick
commands.summary: Apply a specific commit from another branch onto the current branch

options.description: Apply changes without creating a commit
Abort and return to original state

examples.description: Apply a hotfix commit to main branch
Apply a range of commits

categories.name: Branching & Merging
categories.description: Branch strategies, rebasing, and merge workflows

commands.name: git rebase
commands.summary: Reapply commits on top of another base commit or branch

options.description: Interactive rebase of last N commits
Abort the rebase and return to original state
Automatically squash fixup! and squash! commits

examples.description: Interactively squash/reorder last 4 commits
Rebase feature branch onto updated main

commands.note: Never rebase commits that have been pushed to shared branches. Use for local cleanup only.

commands.name: git bisect
commands.summary: Binary search through commit history to find which commit introduced a bug

options.description: Mark current commit as bad (has the bug)
End bisect and return to original branch

examples.description: Start bisect to find regression
examples.code: git bisect start && git bisect bad HEAD && git bisect good v2.1.0

examples.description: Automate bisect with a test script

categories.name: GitHub CLI (gh)
categories.description: Manage PRs, issues, workflows, and releases from the terminal

commands.name: gh pr
commands.summary: Create, view, review, and merge pull requests

options.description: Open a new PR (prompts for details)
List open PRs in the repo
Check out the branch of PR #N locally
Submit an approval or change request

examples.description: Create a PR with title and body
Check out a PR branch for local testing
Merge PR with squash

commands.name: gh workflow
commands.summary: View and trigger GitHub Actions workflows

options.description: List all workflows in the repo

examples.description: Trigger a deploy workflow on a specific branch
examples.code: gh workflow run deploy.yml --ref main -f environment=production

examples.description: List recent runs for a workflow

commands.name: gh run
commands.summary: View and manage GitHub Actions workflow runs

options.description: View details of a specific run

examples.description: Watch a running workflow live
Re-run only the failed jobs of the last run
Download artifacts from run #1234567890

options.description: Upload an asset to an existing release

examples.description: Create a release with auto-generated notes
Upload a binary to an existing release
